title: porophyllum ruderale subsp. macrocephalum
intro: Porophyllum ruderale subsp. macrocephalum, also known as Mexican dandelion or Taraxacum officinale, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the Americas. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural diuretic and digestive aid, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Porophyllum ruderale subsp. macrocephalum include its potential to help lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of kidney stones, and alleviate symptoms of menopause and arthritis. Porophyllum ruderale subsp. macrocephalum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues.
